title: Tiny Plant Trick Could Help Grow More Food
summary: Scientists found a cool trick in a small plant called hornwort that might help us grow more food like wheat and rice. Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis, but an enzyme called Rubisco is slow and gets confused by oxygen, which wastes energy. Hornworts have a special protein called RbcS-STAR that makes Rubisco stick together in clusters, helping it work better. This is like a natural carbon concentrating system. Since hornworts are more similar to crops than algae, this trick could be easier to copy into food plants to make them grow faster and produce more food for people.

background_read: Plants need sunlight to grow and make food through photosynthesis. Rubisco is a key part of this process, but it doesn't work very well because it's slow and gets mixed up with oxygen. Scientists are looking for ways to make Rubisco better so crops like wheat and rice can produce more food. Hornworts are small plants that have a natural way to improve Rubisco by using a special protein. This could help scientists copy the trick into food crops to feed more people around the world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from universities like Cornell and Edinburgh
WHAT: Found a protein in hornworts that makes Rubisco stick together to work better
WHERE: In hornwort plants and labs
WHY: To help crops grow more food efficiently

perspectives.description: Scientists are excited because this trick could make farming better and help fight hunger.
